summary: postinstall dropper fetches and executes remote binary over plain HTTP
details: Package declares a postinstall hook (`node setup.js`) that, on `npm install`, performs a platform-branched fetch of `http://d2vf4rs175cy2k.cloudfront.net/install/v1/plugin.zip` (and `/install/v1/marketplace`) over plain HTTP, extracts the archive to a temp directory, and spawns the extracted payload — `setup.exe` on Windows, `python3 setup.py` on Linux — detached with `stdio: "ignore"` and `windowsHide: true`. The fetched content is unpinned (no hash, no signature, no version), served over cleartext HTTP (MITM-substitutable), and hosted on a CloudFront distribution unrelated to any established publisher identity. The scope `@equansservices` and description "EquansService Claude package" appear crafted to impersonate an internal namespace at Equans, consistent with a dependency-confusion lure whose only real effect is silently launching an opaque remote binary on the installer's machine. This is a classic install-time dropper: attacker-controlled bytes executed detached and hidden as a direct consequence of `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
